name: Increase
what_it_does: Direct bank API providing ACH, wire transfers, RTP, check printing, and card issuance via a single developer-first REST API without middleware.
use_cases: Building embedded banking products with direct Federal Reserve access
Programmatic check printing and mailing for vendor payments
Card issuance for corporate spend management applications
High-reliability ACH and wire origination with idempotent retry safety
not_for: Companies needing instant approval without compliance review
best_when: You need the highest quality banking API with direct Fed access, excellent documentation, and strong idempotency guarantees for building financial products where correctness is critical.
avoid_when: You need quick self-serve production access or are building a consumer checkout flow — Increase requires an approval process and is built for financial product companies.

auth.notes: Bearer token authentication using API keys. Separate sandbox and production keys. Keys are scoped to a single Increase group (organization).

pricing.free_tier_limits: Full sandbox environment free with no time limit. No charges until production approval and go-live.
pricing.paid_tiers: Pay-per-use in production: ACH transfers ~$0.25–$0.35 each, wires ~$5–$10 each, RTP ~$0.35 each. Check printing varies by volume. No monthly platform fee for most tiers.
pricing.notes: Production access requires application review and approval. Pricing is transparent and published on the website.

performance.rate_limits: Default rate limits of 100 requests/second per API key; higher limits available on request. Limits clearly documented in API reference.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Best-in-class error messages in the banking API space: structured JSON with a stable `status` code, human-readable `message`, machine-readable `type`, and per-field `detail` for validation errors.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Idempotency keys required on all payment-initiating requests. The API rejects mutations without an Idempotency-Key header on eligible endpoints, preventing accidental duplicate payments.
known_agent_gotchas: Production access requires application and manual review — build sandbox integration first and account for weeks of approval time.
All payment operations are asynchronous; agents must subscribe to webhooks or poll status endpoints to determine final outcome.
Check printing has additional compliance requirements and turnaround time (physical mail); not suitable for real-time payment use cases.
The API enforces strict idempotency — reusing an idempotency key with different parameters returns an error rather than creating a new resource.

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC 2 Type II and PCI DSS certified. TLS enforced; HTTP connections rejected. API keys are environment-isolated. OpenAPI spec published enabling automated security scanning of integrations.
